topic.name: Marine Resource Management
topic.description: The documents focus on the management, protection, and regulation of marine resources and sanctuaries in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
Prohibits the conduct of aquaculture within Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary.
Document 3: Conduct an evaluation of vessel discharges
Requires promulgation of a final rule establishing enforceable uniform national discharge standards.
Document 6: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
Increase Council membership from 21 to 23 members.
Document 8: Authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts
Revise the criteria for project approval.

topic.name: Health and Workforce Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care education, workforce training, and access to health services, particularly for underserved populations.

Document 1: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to schools of nursing
to educate nurses to serve in rural areas and areas of nurse specialty shortage
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Labor to make certain Fund allocations to states
to provide subsidized employment to unemployed, low-income adults
Document 4: directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a direct care workforce monitoring program
to improve the recruitment, retention, and education of the direct care workforce
Document 8: requires a report on demonstration projects to examine alternatives for expanding care for veterans in rural areas
Document 10: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to make grants to community-based organizations in low-income communities
to expand the inventory of fresh fruits and vegetables available for residents

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and regulations concerning the exploration, development, and production of oil and gas resources in offshore areas, particularly the Outer Continental Shelf and Arctic regions.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coast of New Jersey.
moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 2: States that a Bureau of Land Management (BLM) drilling permit shall not be required under either the Federal Oil and Gas Royalty Management Act of 1982 or the Code of Federal Regulations for an action occurring within an oil and gas drilling or spacing unit.
Retains the right of the federal government to receive royalties from the production of federal minerals within the unit.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement a competitive leasing program for the exploration, development, and production of the oil and gas resources on the Coastal Plain of Alaska.

Amends the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act of 1980 to repeal the prohibition against leasing or other development leading to production of oil and gas from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR).
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct offshore oil and gas Lease Sale 220 on the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) not later than one year after enactment of this Act.
Prohibits the Secretary from making any lease tract available if the President determines that it would create an unreasonable conflict with military operations.
Document 5: declares that it is the policy of the United States that the Arctic Ocean should be managed for the best interests of the people of the United States, including by keeping fossil fuels in the ground to avoid the dangerous impacts of climate change.

prohibit the Department of the Interior from issuing or renewing a lease or any other authorization for the exploration, development, or production of oil, natural gas, or any other mineral in the Arctic Ocean.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the Proposed Final Outer Continental Shelf Oil & Gas Leasing Program (2012-2017).
Requires the Secretary to conduct each of specified oil and gas lease sales in the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) Planning Areas.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct an oil and gas competitive leasing program in the National Petroleum Reserve, Alaska.

Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee: (1) certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the pertinent federal lands.
Document 8: prohibits the Department of the Interior from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act unless they have been renegotiated to require royalty payments.
Rentals or royalties received by the United States under covered leases must be deposited in the Treasury.
Document 9: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to repeal limitations on oil exports.
Directs the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce to grant licenses to export crude oil.
Document 10: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from granting an oil or gas lease to a bidder conducting oil and gas operations off the coast of Cuba unless the bidder submits to the Secretary: (1) a Cuban oil spill response plan.
Requires the Secretary to implement an oil spill risk analysis and planning process for oil spill response plans for nondomestic oil spills in the Gulf of Mexico.

topic.name: Federal Building Designations and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents primarily discusses the designation of federal buildings and courthouses, along with amendments to existing legislation related to government offices and procedures.

1: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to revise the list of Bureau of Land Management (BLM) Federal Permit Streamlining Pilot Project offices.
Replaces the Miles City, Montana, field office with the Montana/Dakotas State Office, Montana (Billings).
2: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 300 West Second Street in Little Rock, Arkansas, as the 'Morris Sheppard Arnold United States Courthouse.'
3: Temporary Bankruptcy Judgeships Extension Act of 2011 - Extends the temporary office of 30 bankruptcy judgeships authorized or extended under the Bankruptcy Judgeship Act of 1992 and Bankruptcy Judgeship Act of 2005 until applicable vacancies identified in this Act occur in the office of a bankruptcy judge for specified districts.
Prohibits filling specified bankruptcy judge vacancies in such districts occurring more than five years after enactment of this Act.
4: Designates the federal building currently known as Federal Office Building 8, located at 200 C Street SW in the District of Columbia, as the 'Thomas P. O'Neill, Jr. Federal Building.'
5: This bill designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 719 Church Street in Nashville, Tennessee, as the 'Fred D. Thompson Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4320 Blue Parkway in Kansas City, Missouri, as the 'Wallace S. Hartsfield Post Office Building.'
8: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 501 East Court Street in Jackson, Mississippi, as the 'R. Jess Brown United States Courthouse.'
9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 10799 West Alameda Avenue in Lakewood, Colorado, as the 'Felix Sparks Post Office Building.'
10: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1265 Hurffville Road in Deptford Township, New Jersey, as the 'First Lieutenant Salvatore S. Corma II Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Accountability
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing legal accountability, transparency, and protections within the judicial and administrative systems.

Document 1: prohibits the Firearms Trace System database from being immune from legal process
may be used, relied on, or disclosed in any manner
Document 2: requires the court to impose an appropriate sanction on any attorney, law firm, or party that has violated the rule
removes a provision that prohibits filing a motion for sanctions if the challenged paper is withdrawn
Document 3: excludes from the definition of 'immigrant' a nonimmigrant alien who files a petition for status
authorizes the Secretary to permit an alien to temporarily remain in the United States
Document 4: requires the Supreme Court to promulgate a code of ethics for the Justices
includes the five canons of the Code of Conduct for United States Judges
Document 5: requires the NSA to have access to business records
amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act
Document 6: requires the Secretary of the Interior to publish the complaint
authorizes the court to award litigation costs to any party
Document 7: expands the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board
permits the Board to investigate whistleblower complaints
Document 8: prohibits specified provisions preempting state and local requirements for medical devices
makes this Act effective as if it were included in the Medical Device Amendments of 1976
Document 9: waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' rights
provides increased liquidated damages against state or private employer violations
Document 10: extends provisions granting roving electronic surveillance authority
allows the recipient of a nondisclosure order to request judicial review

topic.name: Medicare Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving Medicare policies and regulations.

Document 1: Medicare Orthotics and Prosthetics Improvement Act of 2012
modifies the Medicare payment rules for orthotics and prosthetics
Document 2: Healthcare Eligibility for Late Participants (HELP) in Medicare Part D Act of 2007
waive the late enrollment penalty under Medicare part D
Document 3: Medicare Ambulance Payment Extension Act
provide an additional 5% increase in Medicare payments for ground ambulance services
Document 4: Medicare and Medicaid Fighting Fraud and Abuse to Save Taxpayers' Dollars Act
prohibit sponsors of prescription drug plans from paying claims for prescription drugs that do not include the valid National Provider Identifier
Document 5: Seniors' Medication Copayment Reduction Act of 2009
test Value-Based Insurance Design methodologies for Medicare beneficiaries
Document 6: Accessing Medicare Therapies Act of 2013
require incurred costs to include the negotiated price of a covered part D drug
Document 7: Choice in Healthcare Act
establish a demonstration program under which Medicare and Medicaid eligible beneficiaries are given the option of purchasing qualifying health benefits coverage
Document 8: Quality Data, Quality Healthcare Act of 2015
use of certain data by qualified public or private entities to evaluate the performance of service providers
Document 9: Medicaid Enhancement and Emergency Job Creation Act of 2011
extend through FY2012 the increase in the federal medical assistance percentage
Document 10: Fighting Medicare Payment Fraud Act of 2009
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend the number of days in which Medicare claims are required to be paid

topic.name: Employee Benefits and Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing employee benefits, compensation, and protections in the context of unemployment, retirement, and health care.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing expenses and claims to increase to $20,000: allowed unsecured claims in the fourth order of priority (wages, salaries, or commissions)
Includes among prerequisites for confirmation of a business reorganization bankruptcy plan provision for recovery of damages payable for the rejection of a collective bargaining agreement
Document 2: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to revise the formula for computation of the primary insurance amount
Prescribes a formula for an increase in OASDI benefit amounts for beneficiaries on account of long-term eligibility
Document 3: Declares that the purpose of this Act is to keep Americans working by strengthening and expanding short-time compensation programs
Requires the maintenance of health and retirement employee benefits will not be affected by participation in the program
Document 4: Requires restoration of such benefits, at the same rate, upon the dissolution of the remarriage by death, divorce, or annulment
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to increase the rates of veterans' disability compensation
Requires each such increase to be the same percentage as the increase in benefits provided under title II of the Social Security Act
Document 6: Prohibits federal funds from being used to make payments of unemployment compensation to an individual whose resources in the preceding year were at least $1 million
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act to extend emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend federal payments to states
Document 8: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act to extend the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program through December 31, 2010
Extends until January 1, 2011, federal-state agreements increasing regular unemployment compensation payments to individuals
Document 9: Excludes from an employee's gross income employer-provided accident and health plan benefits extended to a domestic partner
Allows self-employed individuals a tax deduction for the health insurance costs of their eligible beneficiaries
Document 10: This bill amends title II of the Social Security Act to increase from $255 to $1,000 the lump-sum death payment

topic.name: Student Loan Reform and Assistance
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving student loan processes, refinancing options, and financial aid transparency for borrowers.

Document 1: Private Education Loan Debt Swap program
refinance the private education loans of certain borrowers
Document 2: prohibit postsecondary educational institutions from using revenues derived from federal educational assistance funds for advertising
protecting financial aid for students and taxpayers
Document 3: allow an exclusion from gross income for the payment of an employer, either to an employee or a lender, of any indebtedness of an employee under a qualified education refinance loan
protection in the event of unemployment or financial hardship
Document 4: minimum requirements for the net price calculator
average yearly price actually charged to first-time, full-time undergraduate students
Document 6: revise specified regulations regarding student loan repayment deferment
deferment available to such borrowers for the duration of postgraduate medical or dental internship
Document 8: eliminate tuition and required fees at public institutions of higher education
modify provisions related to interest rates on federal student loans
Document 9: appoint a Private Education Loan Ombudsman
receive, review, and attempt to resolve informally complaints from borrowers of private education loans
Document 10: bar the Secretary of Education from issuing new regulations related to the prohibition against provision of incentive compensation
penalizes IHEs that substantially misrepresent

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety, accountability, and operational efficiency in the aviation industry.

Document 1: Requires airlines to handle passenger complaints and provide essential services during delays.
Establishes procedures for passenger exit during long delays.
Document 2: Requires FAA to develop procedures for safe drone operation in national airspace.
Document 3: Authorizes modernization of C-130 fleet to meet FAA regulations.
Document 4: Encourages airport master plans to enhance customer service and accessibility.
Document 5: Mandates FAA research on child safety devices during flights.
Document 6: Requires FAA to limit negative impacts of flight paths on communities.
Document 7: Requires air carriers to report agreements affecting revenue sharing.
Document 8: Extends excise taxes on aviation fuels and airport improvement project funding.
Document 9: Directs FAA to plan for reducing commercial flight delays.
Document 10: Prohibits compensation for essential air service to communities near major airports.

topic.name: Justice and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing public safety, justice, and support for victims of crime.

Document 1: Transfers the Office of Dispute Resolution in the Department of Justice to the Office of Legal Policy.
Enacts into law H.R. 2342 (Center to Advance, Monitor, and Preserve University Security Safety Act of 2011 or CAMPUS Safety Act of 2011).
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to make annual grants to the A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center.
Document 3: Reauthorizes the Emmett Till Unsolved Civil Rights Crime Act of 2007 and expands the responsibilities of the Department of Justice.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Justice (DOJ) to reimburse state and local law enforcement agencies for costs associated with the armed occupation of the Malheur National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 5: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education to designate an independent advocate for campus sexual assault prevention and response.
Document 6: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act to require each institution to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment.
Document 7: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to revise and expand requirements for the DOJ grant program for offender reentry demonstration projects.
Document 8: Establishes an Elder Justice program under title XX of the Social Security Act.
Document 9: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act to include certain law enforcement officers serving private institutions of higher education.
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to require a court to order restitution to the victim of a federal offense.

topic.name: Clean Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting clean energy, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and enhancing energy efficiency in the United States.

Document 1: Calls for the enactment of legislation to improve the economy and the security of the United States by reducing U.S. dependence on foreign and unsustainable energy sources
requiring reductions in emissions of greenhouse gases (GHG) in the United States
Document 2: further the deployment of electric drive vehicles and the development of grid services using them
installation of 12,000 charging stations at U.S. Postal Service facilities
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by requiring the Administrator of the Energy Information Administration
the estimated annualized volume of biofuel production for such facility for the current year
Document 4: produces at least 25 million gallons per year of liquid aviation turbine fuel or blending component
has at least 50% less lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions than petroleum
Document 5: define 'fuel products' to mean petroleum products and alternative fuels, including ethanol and biodiesel
renamed the SPR Petroleum Account as the SFR Fuel Account
Document 6: classify as three-year property for depreciation purposes
certain solar energy property
Document 7: allow a new tax credit for investment in projects for manufacturing alternative energy equipment
extend the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
Document 9: continue the maximum dollar amount of the tax credit for new qualified fuel cell motor vehicles
allow such credit for a vehicle which is manufactured primarily to carry or tow loads or materials for commercial or industrial purposes

topic.name: Labor Rights and Election Integrity
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to labor rights, employee protections, and the integrity of electoral processes in the United States.

Document 1: Mail-In Ballot Tracking Act of 2007
require each state and jurisdiction which transmits ballots by mail to implement appropriate procedures to ensure that the ballots may be tracked.
Document 2: Servicemember Employment Protection Act of 2012
protects under USERRA an individual who is absent from employment in order to receive medical or dental treatment for an injury or illness incurred or aggravated in the line of duty.
Document 3: Nurse and Health Care Worker Protection Act of 2015
requires the Department of Labor to establish a standard on safe patient handling, mobility, and injury prevention.
Document 4: require site-controlling employers to keep a site log for all recordable injuries and illnesses
to ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Document 5: Rewarding Achievement and Incentivizing Successful Employees Act
declares that an employer can pay higher wages than a collective bargaining agreement provides for.
Document 6: Voter Confidence and Increased Accessibility Act of 2009
revises requirements for the audit capacity of voting systems.
Document 7: Safeguarding Elections for our Nation's Troops through Reforms and Improvements Act
requires the chief election official of a state to report on the number of absentee ballots validly requested by absent uniformed services and overseas voters.
Document 8: Working Families Flexibility Act
authorizes an employee to request a temporary or permanent change in the terms or conditions of employment.
Document 9: Truth in Employment Act of 2009
provides that nothing in specified prohibitions against unfair labor practices shall be construed as requiring an employer to employ any person.
Document 10: Offshore Oil and Gas Worker Whistleblower Protection Act of 2010
prohibits an employer from discharging, discriminating, or retaliating against employees who report violations or unsafe conditions.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving health care services and benefits for veterans, including mental health care, dental insurance, and specialized treatments.

Document 1: Mental Health Care Provider Retention Act of 2016 allows individuals transitioning from DOD to VA to continue treatment with their DOD provider.
The VA shall reimburse DOD for rendered services that otherwise would have been furnished by the VA.
Document 2: Veterans and Survivors Dental Insurance Act of 2008 authorizes dental insurance for veterans enrolled in the VA system.
Document 3: Veterans Back and Spinal Therapy Act directs the VA to establish a pilot program for non-invasive treatment of spinal injuries.
Document 4: Access to Veteran-Centered Community Care Act ensures veterans receive care at non-VA facilities if they have been waiting too long for appointments.
Document 5: Filipino Veterans Assistance Act provides benefits to Filipino veterans who served with the U.S. Armed Forces.
Document 10: Prioritizing Veterans Access to Mental Health Care Act amends previous legislation to ensure timely mental health care access for veterans.

topic.name: Energy Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, safety, and environmental considerations of energy production and infrastructure, including nuclear, gas, and hydropower sectors.

Document 1: Revises federal pipeline safety requirements
authorize states to enforce state requirements for the safe construction and operation of underground gas storage wellbores
Document 2: amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954
require a Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) licensee to submit to the NRC a post-shutdown decommissioning activities report
Document 3: authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline
deems the Final Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement regarding the pipeline
Document 4: amends the Natural Gas Act and the Energy Policy Act of 2005
repeal federal regulatory oversight over companies engaged in the importation or exportation of natural gas
Document 5: authorizes the Secretary of Energy to barter, transfer, or sell uranium
requires eligible entities to obtain a combined operating license for a reactor approved by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
Document 6: directs the Department of State to request that the International Joint Commission review the long-term impacts
examine the conclusions reached in the Environmental Assessment Report
Document 7: designates and empowers the Secretary of State to receive all applications for permits for the construction, connection, operation, or maintenance
prescribes requirements and procedures for granting a permit
Document 9: amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978
increase from 5,000 to 10,000 kilowatts the size of small hydroelectric power projects
Document 10: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require states to prohibit the underground injection of fluids or propping agents
conduct testing and report data in accordance with this bill

topic.name: Federal Crime Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at establishing penalties and regulations for various federal crimes including drug offenses, public corruption, and firearm regulations.

Document 1: Inhumane Trapping Prevention Act - Makes it unlawful to...
Prescribes criminal penalties for violations.
Document 2: Counterfeit Drug Prevention Act of 2009 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish criminal penalties...
Increases the term of imprisonment to any term of years or for life if the use of a counterfeit drug is the proximate cause of a consumer's death.
Document 3: Clean Up Government Act of 2009 - Amends the federal criminal code to...
increase prison terms for theft or bribery concerning programs receiving federal funds...
Document 5: Food Safety Enforcement Act of 2010 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to impose a fine and/or prison term...
for knowingly violating such Act with respect to food that is misbranded or adulterated.
Document 6: Holding Individuals Accountable and Deterring Money Laundering Act...
impose a 20-year maximum prison term for individuals who facilitate evasion of an anti-money laundering program.
Document 7: Jane's Law - Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or a prison term...
for any person who travels in interstate or foreign commerce to evade compliance with a court-ordered property distribution.
Document 8: Imprisonment for Tax Targeting of Americans Act of 2013 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for a mandatory fine and prison term...
for unauthorized disclosure of tax return and return information.
Document 9: Assault Weapons Ban and Law Enforcement Protection Act of 2007 - Reinstates for ten years repealed criminal provisions regarding assault weapons...
Sets forth penalties for violations.
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit firearm licensees from manufacturing...
a semiautomatic pistol that is not capable of microstamping ammunition.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and transparency in government operations and protecting consumer rights.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies to ensure safety in flood and drainage systems for children.
Mandates compliance with safety measures as a condition for federal funding.
Document 2: Deems submission of information by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs as a covered disclosure.
Directs the Secretary to provide information to relevant congressional committees.
Document 3: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining personal information through fraudulent means.
Requires data brokers to ensure accuracy of personal information they collect.
Document 4: Prohibits use of Social Security numbers on government-issued identification cards.
Encourages private entities to avoid using Social Security numbers for identification.
Document 5: Prohibits deceptive statements in healthcare marketing.
Requires disclosure of applicable healthcare licenses in advertisements.
Document 6: Extends the Child Safety Pilot Program for background checks on volunteers.
Document 7: Requires consumer reporting agencies to include credit scores in free annual reports.
Document 8: Directs USDA to verify employment and income for housing assistance programs.
Sets rules for information disclosures related to housing assistance.
Document 9: Amends the Paperwork Reduction Act to limit fines on small businesses for first-time violations.
Encourages correction of violations without penalties under certain conditions.
Document 10: Requires franchisors to disclose financial performance information to prospective franchisees.
Mandates transparency in franchise operations related to loan qualifications.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax relief, credits, and incentives for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: suspension of the taxable income limitation on percentage depletion for oil and natural gas
marginal properties
Document 2: allow individual taxpayers the full amount of the making work pay tax credit
reduction in the employment and self-employment tax
Document 3: tax credit for differential wage payments to active duty members of the Uniformed Services
make such credit permanent
Document 4: allow individual taxpayers with adjusted gross incomes of less than $75,000 a tax credit for 10% of their income tax
eliminate income taxes for individual taxpayers with adjusted gross incomes of less than $12,500
Document 5: extend through December 31, 2017, the income tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property
Document 6: volunteers who use their automobiles for the benefit of a charitable organization
exclude from their gross income reimbursements for their automobile operating expenses
Document 7: new business-related tax credit for 25% of the equity investment
small business concern that is engaged in a high technology or biotechnology trade
Document 8: tax credit for railroad track maintenance
expand the types of maintenance expenditures eligible for such credit
Document 9: inclusion in gross income for income tax purposes of employee compensation deferred under a nonqualified deferred compensation plan
nonqualified foreign corporation
Document 10: allow taxpayers to withdraw amounts from their tax-exempt pension and retirement plans
without incurring the 10% penalty otherwise imposed on such withdrawals

topic.name: Environmental Protection and Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at environmental protection, flood management, and the control of invasive species in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act to establish a grants program for redevelopment activities for brownfield sites.
redevelopment activities for brownfield sites and abandoned, idled, and underused industrial, commercial, or housing structures
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Army to establish a program to make grants to states to carry out harbor maintenance and deepening projects.
coordinate all activities of the Department of Defense relating to harbor maintenance activities
Document 3: Amends the Federal Land Assistance, Management, and Enhancement Act to include within the purpose of FLAME Wildlife Suppression Reserve Funds to cover the costs of burn area responses, including flood prevention.
Designates the FEMA Administrator as the Chair of the Task Force.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize appropriations for coastal recreation water quality monitoring and notification grants.
reauthorize appropriations to carry out that Act through FY 2012.
Document 5: Grants the consent and approval of Congress to the interstate forest fire protection compact for the Great Plains region.
Document 6: Requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to expedite the completion of the Coastal Texas Protection and Restoration Study.
evaluate the feasibility of developing a comprehensive plan for flood risk management.
Document 7: Requires the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp.
provide support to state and local governments in carrying out activities designed to slow, and eventually eliminate, the threat posed by such carp.
Document 8: Directs the Chief of the Army Corps of Engineers to revise the Missouri Mainstem Reservoir System Master Water Control Manual.
elevate flood control as the highest priority of authorized purposes of the Corps.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary of the Army to take actions to ensure the immediate closure and ceasing of operation of the locks at the O'Brien Lock and Dam.
prevent the migration of bighead and silver carps into Lake Michigan.
Document 10: Amends the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act to establish aquatic invasive species reduction and prevention requirements for vessels.
Requires a national system of ecological surveys for rapid early detection and monitoring of invasive species.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Policy and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various U.S. legislative measures aimed at regulating trade practices, enhancing economic security, and enforcing compliance with international trade agreements.

Document 1: Directs the Comptroller General of the United States to review certain free trade agreements
Establishes a Congressional Trade Agreement Review Committee.
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority
Includes exchange-rate misalignment by a country as a countervailable subsidy.
Document 3: Authorizes the United State Trade Representative to take certain discretionary trade action against foreign countries
Engage in unreasonable acts, policies, or practices that fail to enforce their environmental laws effectively.
Document 4: Directs the Commissioner of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection to establish educational seminars
Improve the ability of CBP personnel to classify and appraise articles imported into the United States.
Document 5: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to direct the President to impose two or more sanctions
If a person has made an investment that significantly contributed to Iran's ability to develop its petroleum resources.
Document 6: Amends the Defense Production Act of 1950 to provide for national security reviews of transactions
Involving the construction of a new facility in the United States by any foreign person.
Document 7: States that it shall be U.S. policy to undertake measures to deny the government of Cuba financial resources
To engage in activities that threaten U.S. security and other interests.
Document 8: Requires additional tariffs to be imposed on products of any nonmarket economy country
Until the President certifies to Congress that such country is a market economy country.
Document 9: Prohibits a U.S.-based company from paying a financial transaction tax imposed by a foreign country
On any covered financial transaction.
Document 10: Expresses the sense of Congress that U.S. Customs and Border Protection should ensure enforcement of U.S. customs laws
With respect to imports of textile or apparel articles.

topic.name: Federal Research and Development Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal initiatives aimed at enhancing research and development across multiple sectors including resilience to extreme weather, cybersecurity, STEM education, ocean mapping, and energy technologies.

Document 1: establish an interagency working group with cabinet-level representation from all relevant federal agencies
develop a National Extreme Weather Resilience Action Plan.
Document 2: transmit to Congress a cybersecurity strategic research and development plan
develop and annually update an implementation roadmap for such plan.
Document 3: establish a STEM Inclusion Interagency Working Group
strengthen the goals and activities of the ADVANCE program to focus on increasing the recruitment, retention, and success of all women in STEM.
Document 4: develop a coordinated and comprehensive federal ocean and coastal mapping program
convene an ocean and coastal mapping advisory panel consisting of representatives from nongovernmental entities.
Document 5: develop innovative consumer financial products that encourage savings and wealth-creation among active-duty servicemembers
contract for an annual independent program evaluation until its termination.
Document 6: coordinate federal activities on ocean acidification
develop a strategic plan for federal ocean acidification research and monitoring.
Document 7: establish the Advanced Research Projects Administration - Energy (ARPA-E)
promoting revolutionary changes in the critical technologies that would promote energy competitiveness.
Document 8: establish a Global Warming Education Program
broaden the understanding of human induced global warming.
Document 9: provide multiyear grants to consortia to conduct research to create innovative and practical approaches to manage the environmental impacts of electronic devices
report to Congress biennially on the grants provided and the results of research projects.
Document 10: establish a research and development program to provide data and scientific analyses for research on critical minerals
promote the development of the critical minerals and materials industry workforce in the United States.

topic.name: U.S. Humanitarian and Refugee Policy
topic.description: The documents collectively address U.S. policies and legislative measures aimed at providing humanitarian assistance and support for refugees and displaced persons in various regions, particularly in Iraq and Central America.

1: U.S. policy to support efforts by the people of Somalia to achieve peace, economic growth, and democracy.
Authorize the President to support a regional or international peacekeeping force for Somalia.
2: Directs the Secretary of State to provide humanitarian assistance to vulnerable populations in Iraq.
Increases refugee admission limitations for the purpose of admitting Iraqi citizens or nationals who became refugees on or after March 19, 2003.
5: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States must address the violence and humanitarian crisis resulting in the elevated numbers of unaccompanied children, women, and refugees from the Northern Triangle.
Funds are made available for combating criminal violence and corruption and advancing economic development.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Reforms
topic.description: Legislative acts focused on reforming agricultural policy, including support programs, funding, and regulations for producers.

Document 1: Democracy for Dairy Producers Act of 2011 requires referendum procedures for milk producers.
Establishes individual ballot information and referendum alerts for producers.
Document 2: CREATE-21 Act establishes the National Institutes for Food and Agriculture.
Amends agricultural research and education programs.
Document 3: MILC Preservation Act makes payment formula permanent for milk income loss contracts.
Document 4: REFRESH Act amends crop year payment programs and replaces average crop revenue election.
Document 5: Rural Access to Credit Act directs USDA to use commercial lending standards for rural loans.
Document 6: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act to end agricultural direct payments.
Document 7: Amends Energy Policy Act to provide financing opportunities for biorefineries.
Document 8: SUGAR Act eliminates nonrecourse support loans for sugar producers.
Document 9: Crop Insurance Subsidy Reduction Act reduces crop insurance premium subsidy rates.
Document 10: Protect our Prairies Act reduces crop insurance assistance for converted native sod.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indian Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at extending federal recognition and associated rights to various Indian tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Severs the United States relations with the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma until the Cherokee Nation is meeting all of its treaty obligations
Grants any Cherokee Freedman a private right to bring actions for injunctive relief.
Document 2: Establishes in the Treasury the Spokane Tribe of Indians Settlement Fund.
Requires the payment of compensation to the Spokane Business Council for the use of tribal lands.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits furnished to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida.
Makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 5: This bill extends federal recognition to the Clatsop-Nehalem Confederated Tribes of Oregon.
The tribe and its members are eligible to receive all federal services and benefits furnished to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 6: Extends to any Indian tribe the discretion granted under current law only to the Navajo Nation to lease restricted lands without the approval of the Secretary of the Interior.
Document 7: Extends federal recognition to the Indian tribes of Virginia.
Makes each Tribe and tribal members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 8: Extends federal recognition to the Duwamish Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized tribes.
Document 9: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida.
Makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 10: This bill replaces the process for federal recognition of Indian tribes.
Indian groups may only be recognized as an Indian tribe by Congress.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss U.S. legislation aimed at imposing sanctions and establishing policies regarding nations and organizations perceived as threats to U.S. national security and global stability.

1: Maintains certain U.S. restrictions against the government of Syria

Imposes specified export, financial, procurement, banking, and property sanctions on persons that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs.
2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the government of Saudi Arabia must cooperate fully with the United States in the investigation of terror groups
Directs the President to prohibit certain exports to Saudi Arabia and restrict travel of certain Saudi diplomats.
3: the Russian Federation is in material breach of its Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces (INF) Treaty obligations
Prohibits the use of Department of State funds to carry out programs of the U.S.-Russia Bilateral Presidential Commission until the President certifies to Congress.
4: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to withhold U.S. contributions to the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees
Limits, upon certification compliance, U.S. contributions to UNRWA.
5: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to the people of Syria
Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should begin a phased removal of sanctions on a post-Assad Syria.
6: Prohibits the issuance of a license for the export of nuclear material, equipment, or technology to the Russian Federation
Limits such certification's use to not more than two consecutive fiscal years.
7: Directs the Secretary of State to designate Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a foreign terrorist organization
report to Congress on IRGC terrorist activities.
8: Requires the Secretary of State to report to Congress on whether the Haqqani Network meets the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization
States that nothing in this Act may be construed to infringe upon the sovereignty of Pakistan.
9: directs the President, within five days after reaching an agreement with Iran regarding Iran's nuclear program, to transmit to Congress
the adequacy of the agreement's safeguards to ensure that Iran's permitted activities will not be used to further any nuclear-related military or nuclear explosive purpose.
10: Sets forth U.S. policy regarding Ukraine, including support for the sovereignty and territorial integrity of a democratic Ukraine
Authorizes and encourages the President to provide assistance to support democracy and civil society in Ukraine.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at increasing accountability, transparency, and economic impact assessments in the federal regulatory process.

Document 1: bars any major rule proposed by a federal agency from taking effect until a study is conducted on its impact on wage growth and employment
the requirement for conducting a study under this bill may not be fulfilled by a study that is conducted prior to this bill's enactment date
Document 2: requires guidance documents of federal agencies to be considered rules subject to the congressional review process
significant guidance documents are subject to review as major rules, which delays their effective date
Document 3: amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act to revise the regulatory process with respect to small entities
expands judicial review of agency rulemaking to permit small entities to seek judicial review
Document 4: requires Congress to approve all new major regulations
a major rule shall be deemed not to be approved if a joint resolution of approval is not enacted
Document 5: prohibits the Department of Health and Human Services from implementing regulations that would replace ICD-9 with ICD-10
the Government Accountability Office must recommend steps to mitigate the disruption to health care providers
Document 6: requires the President and all executive departments to take actions needed to implement recommendations on intelligence and communications technologies
requires all necessary rulemaking to be concluded within one year
Document 7: requires a federal agency to assign a category based on the highest possible annual effect of a rule on the economy
defines 'significant economic impact' as an annual economic effect of at least $100 million
Document 8: requires FERC to publish a cost-benefit analysis before issuing any decision affecting supply and demand curves
prohibits FERC from issuing a decision if costs will be raised for ratepayers
Document 9: requires the Federal Register to be published electronically and made available for sale or distribution
revises requirements for the filing of documents with the Office of the Federal Register
Document 10: prohibits proposed IRS regulations from taking effect
prohibits funds from being used to finalize or implement proposed regulations

topic.name: Tax Fraud Prevention and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at preventing tax fraud, regulating tax return preparers, and enhancing taxpayer protections.

Document 1: Stolen Identity Refund Fraud Prevention Act of 2016
require the Department of the Treasury and the Internal Revenue Service to take several actions to prevent and respond to tax-related identity theft and tax fraud.
Document 3: Homebuyer Tax Credit Oversight and Accountability Act of 2009

requires taxpayers claiming such credit to attach to their returns a properly executed copy of the settlement statement used to purchase their residence and a certified statement of their eligibility for such credit from the real estate broker.
Document 4: Stop Tax Haven Abuse Act
increase the period for Internal Revenue Service review of tax returns involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions.
Document 6: Taxpayer Protection and Preparer Fraud Prevention Act of 2013
Grants authority to the Secretary of the Treasury to regulate and discipline tax return preparers.
Document 10: No Bonuses for Tax Cheats Act

prohibits the Department of the Treasury from providing any discretionary performance award to any employee of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) with respect to whom there is substantial evidence of misconduct.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to federal budget allocations, appropriations, and fiscal policies.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act to revise the process for exercising the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to make purchases of troubled assets.
Prohibits the Secretary from exercising authority to make such purchases unless a joint resolution approving the plan is enacted into law.
Document 2: Makes appropriations of specified amounts for programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 for FY2007-FY2014.
Requires such appropriated amounts to be expended consistent with pay-as-you-go requirements.
Document 3: Establishes the Re-Build America Trust Fund.
Makes appropriations to the Trust Fund of amounts equivalent to all revenues derived from proceeds from the sale and issuance of such bonds.
Document 6: Amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 to revise sequestration requirements for enforcement of discretionary spending limits.
Establishes discretionary spending limits for FY2012-FY2021.
Document 7: Directs the Department of the Treasury to issue a benefit guarantee certificate to each individual entitled to benefits under title II of the Social Security Act.
Represents the obligation of the federal government to provide for payment of OASDI benefits.
Document 9: Establishes within the Department of Justice the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate to conduct audits and oversight of the compensation of the officers and directors of entities assisted under TARP.
Requires the Advocate to negotiate a reduction in executive compensation obligations as a prerequisite to TARP assistance.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming health care policies, insurance coverage, and consumer protections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) to require any employer contribution...
not through the federal employees health benefit program.
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)...to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion...
Applies such requirement to coverage offered in the individual market.
Document 3: Repeals a provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that imposes an annual fee...
based on net premium income.
Document 4: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010...
impose a $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to cover hormone replacement therapy...
Requires coverage of hormone replacement therapy for menopausal symptoms.
Document 6: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act...
to eliminate the requirement that, to be eligible for foster care maintenance payments...
Document 7: Requires each state insurance commissioner to investigate and take appropriate actions on cases of inadequate notices of cancellations...
Amends the Public Health Service Act to declare that federal requirements...shall not be construed to prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements.
Document 8: This bill applies only: (1) if the Supreme Court determines that the premium tax credit under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act...
This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for individuals with health insurance...
Document 9: Exempts from the minimum essential coverage requirements under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act individuals who request an exemption...
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to declare that federal requirements...shall not be construed to prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements...
Requires the Secretary to determine whether the state insurance commissioner or regulator...

topic.name: Health Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to health care, reproductive rights, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to extend and reauthorize appropriations for programs established under the Combating Autism Act of 2006.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to submit a progress report on activities related to autism spectrum disorders.
Document 2: Subjects to fines and/or imprisonment any physician who knowingly performs an abortion but fails to comply with this Act.
Requires a physician who performs an abortion to have admitting privileges at a local hospital.
Document 3: Allows the exchange of human organs for valuable consideration under pilot programs approved by the Department of Health and Human Services.
Currently, exchanging human organs for valuable consideration is prohibited by the National Organ Transplant Act.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for family planning services grants and contracts under the Public Health Service Act.
Prohibits a group health plan from excluding benefits for prescription contraceptive drugs if the plan provides benefits for other outpatient prescription drugs.
Document 5: Prohibits the use of tissue from a spontaneous or induced abortion in research conducted or supported by the National Institutes of Health.
Research with human fetal tissue must meet informed consent requirements.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for the planning and implementation of a national public outreach campaign for women's preventive health.
Establish a national media campaign as part of such campaign.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop voluntary guidelines to manage food allergy and anaphylaxis in schools.
Allows the Secretary to award matching grants to assist local educational agencies in implementing guidelines.
Document 8: Amends the United States Leadership Against HIV/AIDS Act regarding allocation of bilateral global HIV/AIDS assistance.
Revises provisions concerning treatment, prevention, and care goals for HIV/AIDS.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish the Interagency Prostate Cancer Coordination and Education Task Force.
Developing a comprehensive interagency strategy on prostate cancer research and health care programs.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound before informed consent.
Provides for civil penalties for willful failure to comply.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Efficiency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the efficiency, transparency, and coordination of federal programs and agencies.

Document 1: establishes within the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) the National Diabetes Clinical Care Commission
evaluating and expanding education and awareness to health care professionals regarding clinical practices for the prevention of diabetes
Document 3: establishes the Grace Commission II to conduct a review of cost control in the federal government
develop recommendations regarding opportunities for increased efficiency and reduced costs
Document 6: require each Inspector General to make available on its website a list of any recommendations by such Inspector General that have been open for more than one year
Document 9: coordinate with the heads of federal agencies to eliminate, consolidate, or streamline federal programs and agencies with duplicative and overlapping missions
apply any savings towards deficit reduction

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on national security, military operations, and the management of detainees in the context of U.S. defense and foreign policy.

Document 1: Directs the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to serve as an advocate and liaison for the National Guard.
Requires the Chief to consult with state governors and adjutant generals before any changes are made in National Guard force structure.
Document 2: Prohibits fund use to train the Iraqi Security Forces or transfer arms unless authorized by Congress.
Document 3: Repeals the Authorization for Use of Military Force, effective 180 days after enactment.
Document 4: Authorizes the President to permit telecommunications services involving Cuba.
Repeals or amends specified requirements and prohibitions of prior acts.
Document 5: Prohibits U.S. refugee admission of aliens from countries with terrorist-controlled territory unless specific criteria are met.
Document 6: Reaffirms Guantanamo Bay as a location for the detention of individuals engaged in hostilities against the U.S.
Document 7: Prohibits the transfer or release of any Guantanamo detainee to the U.S. without authorization.
Document 8: Prohibits U.S. assistance for certain facility acquisitions in Iraq with specific exemptions.
Document 9: Prohibits the President from releasing or transferring detainees from Guantanamo without certification to Congress.
Document 10: Prohibits the federal government from using drones to kill U.S. citizens without due process.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the inspection, funding, and safety standards of various transportation infrastructure projects, including pipelines and public transit systems.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to require the person purchasing or operating a gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facility to inspect it
Applies this inspection requirement with respect to newly listed abandoned pipelines.
Document 2: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act
Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to award urbanized area formula grants to finance the operating cost of equipment and facilities for use in public transportation.
Document 3: Expands the urbanized area formula grants program to include public transit projects
Revises grant eligibility requirements for FY2010-FY2015 for such projects in urbanized areas.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to water rights, conservation, and management in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: nonmotorized access and use of the Bolts Ditch headgate
use, maintenance, and repair of the ditch and headgate
Document 2: facilitate hydropower development on the Diamond Fork System
report to certain congressional committees if hydropower production has not commenced
Document 3: water rights of the Tribe to be held in trust by the United States
provide loans to the Tribe for the construction of the rural water system
Document 4: authorize specified additional projects for water conservation and improvement
Secretary of the Interior to conduct or participate in projects
Document 5: design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities for recycled water
Limits the federal share to 25% of project costs
Document 6: participate in design, planning, and construction of facilities to reclaim and reuse water
Limits the federal share of project costs to 25%
Document 7: conduct a study to determine feasibility of water supply and conservation project
improve water management efficiency in the Republican River Basin
Document 8: finalize and publish feasibility study for enlargement of the Los Vaqueros Reservoir
work with the state of California and other stakeholders
Document 9: amend the Definite Plan Report for the Seedskadee Project
planning, design, related preconstruction activities, and construction
Document 10: transfer all funds, assets, and real property held in trust for the San Juan Navajo Indians
promote the educational, health, economic, social, and cultural well-being of all Navajo Indians

topic.name: Environmental Regulations and Emissions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating greenhouse gas emissions, promoting energy efficiency, and managing environmental impacts from various industries.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to distribute among the states 1.5% of the emission allowances
Requires states to use the proceeds from such sales exclusively for the benefit of consumers of home heating oil or propane.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act with respect to emissions from small oil and gas exploration and production-related equipment.
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish cost-effective minimal requirements for the prevention of oil and hazardous substance discharges.
Document 3: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from implementing the final rule entitled 'Federal Implementation Plans: Interstate Transport of Fine Particulate Matter and Ozone'
Requires the Administrator to continue to implement the Clean Air Interstate Rule.
Document 4: Prohibits the head of any federal agency from implementing or enforcing any regulations establishing any carbon dioxide or greenhouse gas emissions reductions.
Any regulation that requires those emission reductions is nullified until certification is made.
Document 5: Declares the purpose of this Act to establish a federal program to reduce US greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions substantially enough by 2050.
Establishes the International Climate Change Commission to determine annually whether a WTO participant country has taken certain action to limit its GHG emissions.
Document 6: Prohibits funding for the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from being used to implement or enforce a cap-and-trade program.
A cap-and-trade program is any regulatory program established after enactment of this bill that provides for the sale, auction, or other distribution of a limited amount of allowances.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a carbon incentives program to achieve supplemental greenhouse gas emissions reductions.
Provide financial incentive payments for activities that increase carbon sequestration and storage.
Document 9: Expresses the sense of Congress with respect to the impacts of climate change on health systems.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to publish a national strategic action plan to assist health professionals to prepare for and respond to the impacts of climate change.

topic.name: Employment Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing and prohibiting discrimination in employment based on factors such as sex, sexual orientation, and religious practices.

Document 1: Amends the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 2: Provides remedies for a civil action for deprivation of rights related to freedom of speech, particularly concerning protests at funerals.
Document 3: Prohibits discrimination against employees for reporting violations of the Oil Pollution Act.
Authorizes employees to seek relief for discrimination in violation of this Act.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to clarify the definition of joint employers in relation to employment conditions.
Document 5: Prohibits exclusion from jury service based on sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 6: Prohibits employers from requiring access to employees' personal social media accounts.
Document 7: Amends the Civil Rights Act to address religious accommodations in the workplace.
Document 8: Declares broader definitions of marriage and spouse for federal benefits.
Document 9: Repeals provisions that restricted recognition of same-sex marriages.
Document 10: Amends the Equal Pay Act to strengthen prohibitions against wage discrimination based on sex.

topic.name: Immigration Policy Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Immigration and Nationality Act aimed at modifying visa eligibility, investor programs, and immigration procedures.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of China eligible for designation as a visa waiver program country.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to allow an alien widow of a U.S. citizen to self-petition for continued immediate relative status.
Document 3: Makes Israeli nationals eligible to enter the United States as nonimmigrant traders and investors under the Immigration and Nationality Act.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make the EB-5 immigrant investor program permanent.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include nationals of Ireland coming to the United States under a treaty of commerce.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to create an E-4 treaty trader visa category for nationals of the Republic of Korea.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit an E-2 alien to apply for immediate employment-based immigrant status.
Document 8: Prohibits a consular officer from issuing a visa to any alien from a country designated by the World Health Organization as having widespread Ebola transmissions.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt certain returning H-2B aliens from annual numerical limitations.
Document 10: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from granting work authorization to aliens unlawfully present in the United States.

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing benefits and support for military veterans and their families.

Document 1: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation.
Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and either military retired pay.
Document 2: Redefines the term 'period of war' for purposes of eligibility for veterans' pension benefits.
Includes any period of active military, naval, or air service not specifically referenced.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a Transition Assistance Advisor program.
Provides professionals in each state to assist members of the Armed Forces in accessing benefits.
Document 4: Revises the effective date for being considered on active duty.
Entitlement to active-duty medical and dental care for members of the reserves.
Document 5: Amends federal bankruptcy law to protect active duty members from bankruptcy dismissal.
Directs a study on the effects of this Act on members of reserve components.
Document 6: Entitles the spouse of a person who dies in the line of duty to educational assistance.
Terminates entitlement after 15 years or upon remarriage.
Document 7: Requires crediting of years of service for members of the reserves awarded the Purple Heart.
Impacts computation of years of service for retired pay.
Document 8: Directs federal agencies to hire qualified veterans for positions.
Includes requirements for appointing no less than 10,000 qualified veterans.
Document 9: Directs the Department of Defense to provide postal benefits to active duty members.
Applies to members serving in hostile fire areas or hospitalized due to service.
Document 10: Authorizes payment of the expense of presenting a U.S. flag to children of members who die in service.

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States for temporary suspensions of duties on various goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain PCBTF with acid acceptor.
Document 2: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on such articles.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on stop valves each designed to be actuated by steam turbine hydraulic control systems.
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on open top, electric indoor grills.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on staple fibers of rayon.
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain smooth nonwoven fiberglass sheets.
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on thermoplastic biodegradable polymer blend.
Document 8: extend the temporary suspension of duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon.
Document 9: suspend temporarily the duty on certain clock movements.
Document 10: suspend temporarily the duty on sports footwear for persons other than men.

topic.name: U.S. Defense and Intelligence Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative acts that authorize appropriations for defense and intelligence-related activities.

Document 1: National Shipbuilding Budget Policy Act - Authorizes appropriations for FY2011-FY2015 for: construction of vessels to meet Navy force requirements.
loan guarantees and commitments for the construction of new vessels to replace and expand the U.S. fleet of commercial vessels.
Document 2: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of: the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI); the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); the Department of Defense (DOD); the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA); the National Security Agency (NSA).
Document 3: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 - Title I: Intelligence Activities - Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); Department of Defense (DOD).
Document 4: Appropriates funds for FY2009 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for: military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force.
family housing construction and related operation and maintenance for the military departments and DOD.
Document 5: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2009 - Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: Office of the Director of National Intelligence; Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); Department of Defense (DOD).
Document 6: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 - Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); Department of Defense (DOD).
Document 8: Appropriates funds for FY2008 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force.
Appropriates funds for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for various veteran-related programs.

topic.name: Federal Communications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating and improving telecommunications services and infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act
allows federal entities to participate in the incentive auction program
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to adopt a final rule
support under the Universal Service Fund Lifeline Assistance Program for qualifying low-income consumers
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to consider regulation of inmate telephone service
prescribing a maximum uniform per-minute rate
Document 4: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 and the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005
extend for approximately four months the deadlines related to the transition from analog to digital television broadcasting
Document 5: Amends the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001
to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to modify the rules authorizing the operation of low-power FM radio stations
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to make it unlawful for any person in the United States to transmit misleading or inaccurate caller identification information
with the intent to defraud or deceive
Document 7: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require public, educational, and government (PEG) channels to be carried in the same channel capacity as local commercial television channels
requires cable operators to carry PEG signals to subscribers without material degradation
Document 8: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to revise the categories of calls prohibited from using any automatic telephone dialing system
prohibits such calls to any telephone number assigned to a cellular service
Document 9: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a satellite carrier to retransmit the digital signals of each noncommercial educational television station
that is located in any local U.S. market
Document 10: amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require intermediate providers to register with the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
comply with service quality standards to be established by the FCC

topic.name: Political Campaign Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on laws and amendments related to the regulation of political campaigns, election processes, and political organizations in the United States.

Document 1: Applies the antitrust laws to a political committee under the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
Prohibits any such political committee for multiple legal entities in the same business.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax exemption of political organizations
Defines 'promotion of social welfare' for purposes of the tax-exemption for social welfare organizations.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the prohibition against churches and other tax-exempt organizations participating in political campaigns.

topic.name: Educational Grants and Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal acts that provide grants and funding to support educational initiatives aimed at improving access, quality, and outcomes for students, particularly those from low-income or underrepresented backgrounds.

Document 1: authorizes matching six-year grants to local educational agencies (LEAs)
support dual enrollment programs
Document 2: directs the Department of Education (ED) to allot matching grants to states
implement high-quality prekindergarten programs for children from low-income families
Document 3: awards grants to states to carry out programs that promote the involvement of elementary and secondary female students in STEM fields
Document 4: requires each state and local educational agency (LEA) to ensure that each homeless child has access to the same free, appropriate public education
Document 5: authorizes competitive matching grants to states for full day voluntary prekindergarten programs
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Education to award renewable competitive grants to expand or replicate successful public charter schools
Document 7: allows matching grants to states for public elementary and secondary school security
Document 8: authorizes competitive grants to establish and administer American Dream Accounts for low-income public school students
Document 9: includes high school students as beneficiaries of the safe routes to school program
Document 10: authorizes renewable five year grants to ensure the academic, physical, social, emotional, and civic development of disadvantaged youth

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, protection, and management of wildlife and natural habitats in the United States.

Document 1: establish the San Francisco Bay National Wildlife Refuge Complex
urban ecosystem wildlife refuge
Document 2: establishment under this Act of the Lesser Prairie Chicken Habitat Preservation Area
protection, conservation, and enhancement of habitat for the lesser prairie chicken
Document 4: permit the taking of such prairie dog under the Endangered Species Act
threat to public health and safety
Document 5: renames the Snake River Birds of Prey National Conservation Area
instrumental in the establishment of this National Conservation Area
Document 8: conserve, protect, and restore nationally significant landscapes
outstanding cultural, ecological, and scientific values
Document 10: provide assistance for projects for the conservation of rare felids and rare canids
Great Cats and Rare Canids Conservation Fund

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and regulatory frameworks affecting financial institutions, particularly bank holding companies and insurance entities.

Document 1: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act with respect to establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements
appropriate federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject to such minimum requirements any person, to the extent that person acts in its capacity as a regulated insurance entity
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets
Defines 'economic benefit' as the difference between actual loan terms offered, debt or equity prices, or asset values
