Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the taxable income of a life insurance company
any non-taxed reinsurance premium
Document 4: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to declare that any regulation, order, or other action of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall not be effective nor enforceable with respect to an entity that is a savings and loan holding company that is also an insurance company
requiring a bank holding company that is an insurance company to serve as a source of financial strength
Document 5: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to authorize the Chairperson of the Financial Stability Oversight Council to issue a stay of, or set aside, any regulation issued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Requires the CFPB Director to consider its impact upon the financial safety or soundness of an insured depository institution
Document 6: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to authorize the Federal Reserve Board to make advances to a special purpose vehicle or a designated corporate entity
Restricts the use of such advances solely to financing the purchase by such a special purpose vehicle
Document 7: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to direct the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to promote the goal of long-term price stability
Directs the Board to include in its semiannual report to Congress the results of the evaluation process
Document 8: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to exempt from its rules regarding management assessment of internal controls
Declares the 'Small Bank Holding Company Statement' of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System applicable to a financial institution
Document 9: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to repeal the contingent conditions under which the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers shall not be established
Reestablishes the NARAB without contingent conditions as a nonprofit corporation
Document 10: Repeals the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
revives or restores those the provisions of law amended by it as if that Act had not been enacted

topic.name: Aviation and Transportation Security
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative measures and policies aimed at enhancing security protocols and technologies in aviation and transportation sectors.

Document 1: expresses the sense of Congress that the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) should increase efforts to address privacy concerns regarding the screening of passengers using advanced imaging technology at airports
requires all advanced imaging technology equipment used at airport checkpoints to be configured so that passenger images are not retained
Document 2: directs the Secretaries of Health and Human Services (HHS) and Agriculture respectively, in coordination with the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS), to designate as 'Tier I agents' those agents and toxins which have clear potential to be used effectively in a biological attack
establish enhanced biosecurity measures for persons that possess, use, or transfer Tier I agents
Document 3: directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Transportation Security Administration [TSA]) to develop and implement a plan for expedited security screening services for a uniformed Armed Forces member
requires the Assistant Secretary to consider certain protocols in developing the plan
Document 4: directs the Federal Air Marshal Service to require federal air marshals hired after the enactment of this Act to complete the criminal investigative training program
requires any air marshal hired before the enactment of this Act who has not completed the program to complete an alternative training program
Document 8: revises federal flight training requirements authorizing a flight instructor, pilot school, or aviation training center to provide training in the operation of certain aircraft to an alien

prescribes the additional requirement that such training may occur only upon receipt of information from the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (TSA) that the individual has been checked against the terrorist watchlist
Document 9: directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (TSA) to establish in the TSA an aviation security advisory committee
requires the Advisory Committee to develop, upon the Assistant Secretary's request, recommendations to improve aviation security

topic.name: Regulatory Improvements in Drug and Food Safety
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing the regulation and safety of drugs and food products in the United States.

Document 1: Expanding and Promoting Expertise in Rare Treatments Act of 2012
promote the efficiency of and inform the review by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) of drugs and biologic products for rare diseases.
Document 2: Patient Safety and Generic Labeling Improvement Act
allows the holder of an approved abbreviated new drug application (generic drug approval) to change the labeling of a drug.
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), at the request of the sponsor of a drug, to expedite the drug's development and review
designate the drug as a breakthrough therapy.
Document 4: Safe and Affordable Drugs from Canada Act of 2015
require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to promulgate regulations within 180 days permitting individuals to import a prescription drug.
Document 5: John Rainey Memorial Safeguard American Food Exports (SAFE) Act
deem equine parts to be an unsafe food additive or animal drug.
Document 6: Child Nicotine Poisoning Prevention Act of 2015
requires any nicotine provided in a liquid nicotine container to be packaged in accordance with the Consumer Product Safety Commission's standards.
Document 7: Reliable and Effective Growth for Regenerative Health Options that Improve Wellness or the REGROW Act
require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to conditionally approve certain cellular therapeutic products.
Document 8: Keeping America's Food Safe Act of 2008
require certification of any food safety laboratory or a sampling service that is analyzing, testing, or collecting samples of imported food.
Document 9: Freedom of Health Speech Act
prohibit considering the content of any publication as advertising regulable under the Act unless the content is intended by a product seller to promote that product's sale.

topic.name: American Legislative Measures
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts focusing on domestic policies, procurement regulations, and military operations.

Document 9: Amends the East Bench Irrigation District Water Contract Extension Act to permit contract extensions.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to solicit proposals for the operation and maintenance of golf courses.

topic.name: Small Business Programs Enhancement
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving and expanding federal support for small business innovation, procurement, and financing initiatives.

Document 1: reauthorize through FY2023 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases the individual small business award levels
Document 2: direct each federal agency to issue research topic solicitations at least twice a year
requires each federal agency conducting an SBIR program to engage with such awardees
Document 3: require the SBA Administrator to establish a process under which a lender provides to the major credit reporting agencies information about the borrower
increase from $7,500 to $10,000 the limit for loans made to intermediaries
Document 4: requires a federal agency to award to a small business concern each acquisition contract of more than $3,000
amends the Small Business Act to prohibit considering an individual having net worth of over $1.5 million as economically disadvantaged
Document 5: allows for the management of each Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization
review agency purchases that are greater than the micro-purchase threshold
Document 6: establish a program to guarantee loans of small businesses that manufacture a clean energy technology
carry out the program in a manner similar to the SBA 7(a) general business loan program
Document 8: authorize the Administrator of the SBA to make grants to small businesses to assist in the commercialization of research
funds received under the second phase of the Small Business Innovation Research Program
Document 10: require the Administrator of the SBA to wait until five years after the program is implemented before conducting a required study
presumes as underrepresented by women-owned small businesses the industries identified in industry codes 11 through 81

topic.name: Rural Housing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving housing conditions and assistance for rural areas and low-income families in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
...has a serious lack of mortgage credit for lower and moderate-income families.
Document 3: This bill amends the Housing Act of 1949 to direct the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to extend rural housing vouchers to any low-income household.
USDA may also establish a Multifamily Housing Revitalization Program for the preservation and revitalization of multifamily housing projects.
Document 4: ...limit to a city or town with under 50,000 inhabitants the meaning of 'rural' with respect to rural lenders.
...presume that the applicant for a residential mortgage loan has a reasonable ability to repay the loan.

topic.name: Federal Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to the conveyance, management, and utilization of federal lands for purposes such as economic development, cultural preservation, and infrastructure projects.

Document 1: convey such property to Ventura County, California, for airport-related purposes
Document 2: authorizes Sealaska Corporation to select and receive conveyance of its remaining Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act land entitlement
requires the Secretary of Interior to complete the conveyance of land to Sealaska within 180 days
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Alaska Railroad Corporation all rights, title, and interests of the United States to certain land
Document 5: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to exchange approximately 20 acres of land within the boundary of the Blue Ridge Parkway
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Summit County, Colorado, all interest of the United States in approximately 40 acres of National Forest System land
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Miami-Dade County, Florida, federally owned land for the purpose of building a fire station
Document 9: directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the administrative jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Navy identified public land
requires the transferred land to be included as part of the Naval Air Weapons Station China Lake
Document 10: requires conveyance of approximately 44 acres of federally owned land administered by the Agricultural Research Service to the city of Ames
